description.en: Milan's towering holiday bread, its dome raised over days by a stiff natural starter alone and studded with candied citrus and raisins. Torn open at Christmas and New Year's tables, it rewards the wait with an impossibly light, fragrant crumb.
description.de: Mailands hoch aufragendes Festtagsgebäck: seine Kuppel wächst über Tage allein durch einen festen Natursauerteig und ist gespickt mit kandierten Zitrusfrüchten und Rosinen. An Weihnachten und Neujahr aufgerissen, belohnt es die Geduld mit einer federleichten, duftenden Krume.
description.es: El elevado pan festivo de Milán, cuya cúpula crece durante días gracias solo a una masa madre firme, salpicado de cítricos confitados y pasas. Se abre a mano en las mesas de Navidad y Año Nuevo, premiando la espera con una miga ligerísima y aromática.
description.fr: Le pain de fête tout en hauteur de Milan, dont le dôme se développe sur plusieurs jours grâce au seul levain naturel, parsemé d'agrumes confits et de raisins secs. Déchiré à Noël et au Nouvel An, il récompense l'attente par une mie légère et parfumée.

origin.note: Milanese in origin, traditionally leavened only with lievito madre, a stiff mother starter refreshed for days before baking. Home and industrial versions sometimes add a little baker's yeast for reliability, but classic artisan formulas stay purely natural.

ingredients.note: mature stiff lievito madre (pasta madre), about 40% hydration, refreshed before use
high-protein (Manitoba-style) flour: 240g in the primo impasto, 60g in the secondo impasto
granulated: 75g in the primo impasto, 66g in the secondo impasto
54g in the primo impasto, 96g in the secondo impasto
72g in the primo impasto, 40g in the secondo impasto, softened
diastatic malt powder; 1 tsp honey may substitute
in the overnight aromatic infusion, with citrus zest
zest of 1 orange, in the aromatic infusion
zest of 1 lemon, in the aromatic infusion
sultanas, folded in with the candied peel
60g candied orange peel plus 30g candied lemon peel

keeping.note: A proper natural-levain crumb stays remarkably soft and fragrant for about two weeks sealed at room temperature; the long fermentation and high sugar and fat content act as natural preservatives.

method.title: Infuse the aromatics
method.summary: Warm honey with orange and lemon zest and vanilla seeds; refrigerate overnight so the flavors steep together.

method.title: Build the first dough
method.summary: Knead the ripe stiff starter with flour and sugar-water until stretchy, then work in butter and egg yolks; ferment about 12 hours at warm room temperature until tripled.

method.title: Build the second dough
method.summary: Knead in more flour and the malt, then gradually add sugar, egg yolks, salt and the aromatic infusion, keeping the dough below 28°C throughout mixing.

method.title: Add butter and fruit
method.summary: Work in the remaining butter in stages, then fold in the raisins and candied peel on the lowest speed just until distributed.

method.title: Shape and mold
method.summary: Rest the dough briefly, shape into balls, and settle each into a tall paper panettone mold pierced near the base with skewers.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Let rise in a warm spot until the dough nears the rim of the mold, roughly 6-8 hours.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Score a shallow cross on top, add a small pat of butter, and bake at 175°C (350°F) for 25-35 minutes.

method.title: Cool upside down
method.summary: Immediately skewer through the base and suspend each loaf upside down for about 12 hours so the tall crumb does not collapse as it cools.

source.author: adapted from Piergiorgio Giorilli's formula
source.adaptation: Method paraphrased and condensed from a two-day primo/secondo impasto process; citrus zest and vanilla pod weights estimated since the source gives them by piece, not grams.
